Calculate latitudinal range

Source: R/ranges.R
latrange.Rd

Calculate latitudinal range

Usage

latrange(coordMat, plot = FALSE, plot.args = NULL)

Arguments

coordMat

2D numeric matrix with two columns: longitudes and latitudes.

plot

Logical, should the result be plotted? Will plot over active plot (as in add=TRUE).

plot.args

List arguments passed to the plotting function: sf::plot.

Value

A list with an estimate and two latitudes: the minimum (southernmost) and maximum (northernmost) latitude.

Examples

# 1. Canvas
hex <- hexagrid(deg=5, sf=TRUE)
#> Selecting hexagrid with tessellation vector: c(2, 4).
#> Mean edge length: 4.993 degrees.
plot(hex, reset=FALSE, xlim=c(-15, 40), ylim=c(25, 63))

# 2. Records
data(pinna)

# just the coordinates
coordMat <- SimpleCoordinates(pinna, long="decimallongitude", lat="decimallatitude")
points(coordMat)

# 3. calculate and visualize
latrange <- latrange(coordMat, plot=TRUE)


# abline(h=latrange$range, lty=2)
